fields.description:
## Context

Agents across DSO workflows create bug tickets with free-form, inconsistent descriptions that make triage harder. The existing ticket CLI accepts any description, which is correct — blocking bug creation with strict format enforcement would cause agents to skip reporting entirely. But without guidance, bug reports lack structured expected/actual behavior, consistent titles, and useful logs. By providing a template with post-creation validation warnings (never gates), a lightweight create-bug skill, and using-dso guidance, agents get three chances to produce high-quality reports without any barrier to tracking issues.

## Success Criteria

- A shared bug report template exists at plugins/dso/skills/shared/prompts/bug-report-template.md with required sections (Expected Behavior, Actual Behavior) and optional sections (Technical Environment, Action History and Workarounds, Logs, Additional Details)
- The Logs section includes guidance to truncate at 30K characters and use ticket comments for overflow
- The template specifies a structured title format: [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] with the component hierarchy (Tool/Skill -> File/Path -> Logical Workflow)
- Post-creation validation prints a warning when the title does not match the [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] pattern, directing the agent to edit the title via the ticket edit command
- Post-creation validation prints a separate warning when the description is missing Expected Behavior or Actual Behavior headers, directing the agent to edit the description via the ticket edit command
- Bugs are always created regardless of format compliance — warnings never block creation
- A lightweight /dso:create-bug skill exists that references the template and the correct ticket command
- Using-dso guidance instructs agents to use the template when creating bugs

## Dependencies

None

## Approach

Three-layer guidance with post-creation validation. A shared template (bug-report-template.md) defines the format. Post-creation validation in the ticket CLI warns on missing title pattern or description headers — warnings only, never blocking. A lightweight create-bug skill references the template. Using-dso guidance provides the third reminder. The Zero Inference rule from the existing protocol research (no root cause speculation, no hindsight bias, no forced debugging, no low-value tickets) is incorporated into the template.

## Research Findings

Discovery session (captured in epic comments) established: structured title format using Component-Behavior pattern, 0-4 priority rubric tied to blocker/workaround criteria, Zero Inference rule prohibiting root cause speculation and hindsight bias, chronological rationalization as a black-box recorder, scenario-specific rules for user-flagged/sub-agent/nitpick bugs, and gating criteria for low-value ticket prevention.
